rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SPENCER HO 2005 SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #9, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/12/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Paragraph 13 of the petition is very difficult to follow and is missing some information. What was the date of the order authorizing the conservator to set aside $12,000? It would be helpful if, rather than narrative form, the fees set aside were shown in a table or chart that showed the date of the order authorizing (and in which case if any were ordered in the trust), the amount ordered, when the amount was set aside (which accounting petition), the date when a fee was paid, and the running/current balance. The Bank of America CD "statement" is not, in fact, a statement but appears to be just a printout from the bank's website: no vesting (title to the account) is shown. (The DHCS also filed a Request for Special Notice on 6/14/21.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/3/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PHEBE CONLEY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Remove Co-Trustee Carlos F. Mcclatchy And Appoint Susan B. Mcclatchy As Successor Co-Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/12/2022 to allow e-filings to be processed. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Respondent must submit the required courtesy copies of the filings in the Examiner in room 202 once they are filed. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 12/27/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF DARLENE J FULLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The conservator paid himself $1,121.06 on 11/5/18 and $990.10 on 4/16/19, purportedly pursuant to the 5/9/19 order. This does not make sense. He has not yet explained or supported these payments. The order dated 5/9/19 authorized, retroactively, his reimbursement to himself of the $2,633.15 reflected in his second accounting. (He requested such retroactive approval in paragraph D of his second accounting petition filed on 10/31/18.) 2) As with the prior accounting some of the attorney's time entries appear to have been incurred to address issues resulting, in part, from the attorney's own errors or oversights. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALBERT WITT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Either Antoher Petition To Approve Status Of Administration, Or Petition For Final Distribution.
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Neither a petition for final distribution nor a report of status with a petition for additional time to administer the estate has been filed since letters were issued to the executor more than 4 years ago. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.
